title: geonoma cuneata subsp. irena
intro: Geonoma cuneata subsp. irena, also known as Irena's geonoma or Irena palm, is a species of flowering plant in the family Arecaceae, specifically a type of palm tree native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant compounds that have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Geonoma cuneata subsp. irena include its potential to reduce inflammation and pain, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against oxidative stress. Geonoma cuneata subsp. irena is traditionally used to prepare herbal remedies, such as infusions and decoctions, which are often consumed as teas or used topically to treat skin conditions.
